questions.question: What is TypeScript best described as?
options: A) A completely separate language from JavaScript
B) A typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JavaScript
C) A runtime environment for JavaScript
D) A JavaScript testing framework

questions.question: What does the `tsc` command do?
options: A) Runs TypeScript code directly in the browser
B) Installs TypeScript packages from npm
C) Compiles TypeScript files into JavaScript files
D) Starts a TypeScript development server

questions.question: Which file extension is used for TypeScript source files?
options: A) .js
B) .jsx
C) .ts
D) .typ

questions.question: What happens to TypeScript type annotations after compilation?
options: A) They are preserved in the output JavaScript
B) They are converted to runtime type checks
C) They are completely removed from the output
D) They are stored in a separate metadata file

questions.question: What is the purpose of tsconfig.json?
options: A) To list all TypeScript files in the project
B) To configure compiler options and project settings for TypeScript
C) To define npm dependencies for the project
D) To store environment variables for TypeScript

questions.question: Which command installs TypeScript globally using npm?
options: A) npm install typescript
B) npm install -g typescript
C) npm install -g tsc
D) npm install ts-compiler

questions.question: What does ts-node allow you to do?
options: A) Compile TypeScript to WebAssembly
B) Run TypeScript files directly without a separate compilation step
C) Debug TypeScript in the browser
D) Convert JavaScript files to TypeScript

questions.question: When does TypeScript perform type checking?
options: A) At runtime in the browser
B) At compile time, before the code runs
C) Both at compile time and runtime
D) Only when explicitly requested with a flag

questions.question: Which of the following is valid TypeScript but NOT valid JavaScript?
options: A) const x = 5;
B) let name: string = 'Alice';
C) console.log('hello');
D) function greet() { return 'hi'; }

questions.question: What does the `strict` option in tsconfig.json do?
options: A) Prevents the use of any third-party libraries
B) Enables a set of strict type-checking options for stronger safety guarantees
C) Forces all variables to be declared with const
D) Disables all JavaScript interoperability

questions.question: What is the output file extension when TypeScript compiles a .ts file?
options: A) .ts
B) .tsx
C) .js
D) .mjs

questions.question: Which tsconfig.json option specifies where compiled JavaScript files are placed?
options: A) "rootDir"
B) "outDir"
C) "baseUrl"
D) "sourceRoot"

questions.question: What will happen if you try to run a .ts file directly with `node myfile.ts`?
options: A) It will run successfully because Node.js supports TypeScript natively
B) It will likely fail due to syntax errors from type annotations
C) It will automatically compile and then run
D) It will run but ignore all type annotations

questions.question: Which of these is a benefit of using TypeScript over plain JavaScript?
options: A) Faster runtime execution speed
B) Smaller bundle sizes
C) Catching type-related bugs before the code runs
D) Built-in database connectivity

questions.question: What does the `target` option in tsconfig.json control?
options: A) Which browser the code will run in
B) The ECMAScript version the output JavaScript should conform to
C) The minimum Node.js version required
D) The TypeScript version to use for compilation

questions.question: Is every valid JavaScript program also valid TypeScript?
options: A) Yes, because TypeScript is a superset of JavaScript
B) No, JavaScript syntax is completely different
C) Only if the JavaScript uses ES6+ syntax
D) Only if you add type annotations to the JavaScript

questions.question: What does `tsc --init` do?
options: A) Initializes a new npm project
B) Creates a default tsconfig.json file in the current directory
C) Installs TypeScript dependencies
D) Compiles all TypeScript files in the current directory

questions.question: Which of the following TypeScript errors is caught at compile time?
options: A) Division by zero
B) Assigning a string to a variable declared as number
C) An infinite loop
D) A network request timeout

questions.question: What is the role of type inference in TypeScript?
options: A) It converts types to strings at runtime
B) It automatically determines the type of a value without an explicit annotation
C) It forces every variable to have a type annotation
D) It generates documentation from types

questions.question: Which tsconfig.json option specifies the root directory of source files?
options: A) "outDir"
B) "include"
C) "rootDir"
D) "sourceMap"

questions.question: What does enabling `sourceMap` in tsconfig.json provide?
options: A) A list of all source files in the project
B) Mapping files that link compiled JS back to original TS for debugging
C) A visual map of the project structure
D) Faster compilation speed

questions.question: Which command compiles a specific TypeScript file?
options: A) tsc compile myfile.ts
B) tsc myfile.ts
C) typescript myfile.ts
D) node --tsc myfile.ts

questions.question: What does `tsc --watch` do?
options: A) Watches for security vulnerabilities in the code
B) Compiles once and exits
C) Monitors files for changes and recompiles automatically
D) Opens a visual dashboard for TypeScript errors

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of TypeScript?
options: A) Static type checking
B) Interfaces and type aliases
C) Runtime type enforcement in production
D) Generics

questions.question: If TypeScript finds a type error during compilation, what happens by default?
options: A) The JavaScript output is still generated, but a warning is shown
B) Compilation is completely halted and no output is produced
C) The type error is silently ignored
D) The error is logged to a separate error file
